heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 02 January 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 1 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.6.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 0 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (202°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 6.4°C), outside temperature (up 5.7°C), and inside temperature (up 2.5°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 7%) and rainfall (up 0.6 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 434 m) and pressure (up 11 mbar).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 494 m), inside temperature (up 1.8°C), feels like temperature (down 4.7°C), and outside temperature (down 4.0°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 434.5m lower than yesterday, 493.6m lower than the past week, 435.0m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 708m to a maximum of 1548m, averaging 690.2m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1183.9m and 1125.3m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Expect overcast skies with low cloud.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will drop to chilly levels. Outside; chilly temperatures are forecasted. Also, dry and less humid conditions and very little to no wind will be present. A few light raindrops may fall, overcast skies with low cloud, and it’s dim outside. Low pressure may bring unsettled weather.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; if you have washing out then bring it in as it will probably get damper overnight.
